channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Federato Expands Into APAC, Japan
item.description: (Image credit: IIR/Gemini.) Federato (San Francisco) has expanded into Asia-Pacific and Japan, opening an office in Sydney and naming Roland Slee Regional VP, Asia Pacific and Japan. Federato says its APAC and Japan expansion is intended to bring its insurance platform to insurers seeking to apply AI more directly to underwriting and claims operations. “Federato is fully committed to serving insurers across APAC and Japan, and Roland Slee is key to this effort,” comments Will Ross, Co-Founder and CEO, Federato.

item.title: Aon Names Mike Reid EMEA Facultative Leader
item.description: “Mike is a great example of this approach, and we are excited to have him lead our EMEA Facultative Reinsurance team.” “Aon is a recognized leader in the facultative sector and this role is a compelling opportunity to apply my experience and relationships to deliver innovative solutions to clients,” comments Reid. (Image credit: IIR/Gemini.) Aon (Dublin) has appointed Mike Reid EMEA CEO of Facultative Reinsurance, effective July 1, 2026. Mike Reid, EMEA CEO, Facultative Reinsurance, Aon.

item.title: PE-backed Insurance Broker Hub International Files Confidentially for U.S. IPO
item.description: Private equity-backed insurance broker Hub International announced on Friday that it has confidentially filed for an initial public offering in the United States. Within the insurance sector, brokers have dominated recent listings, accounting for six of the 16 largest IPOs since 2021, led by Ryan Specialty Holdings, according to S&P Global. Analysts note that insurance brokers face less volatility than underwriters when going public.

item.title: More Americans Expected to Travel Over July 4th Holiday
item.description: Nearly 72.2 million Americans will travel at least 50 miles from home for Independence Day between Saturday, June 27 and Sunday, July 5. Independence Day Travelers by Mode of Transportation By Car: AAA projects 61.4 million people will travel by car over the July 4th week, nearly the same number as last year when 61.3 million travelers took road trips. This year’s domestic travel forecast surpasses last year’s record of 71.8 million travelers but is smaller than recent year-over-year gains, with cruises the only mode of transportation seeing significant gains.

item.title: Economic and insured losses from June US SCS outbreak likely to reach low single-digit billions: Aon
item.description: Preliminary estimates indicate that combined economic and insured losses from severe convective storm (SCS) outbreaks in the United States between June 18-25, 2026, will likely land in the low single-digit billions USD, according to broking group Aon’s Weekly Cat Report. Another key factor for this severe weather was the remnants of Tropical Storm Arthur, which produced a regional tornado outbreak with numerous weak-to-moderate tornadoes in Louisiana and Mississippi, while a subsequent episode from June 21–23 featured stronger tornadoes and a long-track derecho from Kansas into Oklahoma and Arkansas. Between June 21–23, a separate outbreak of severe weather and tornadoes occurred across parts of Kansas, Illinois, and Indiana.

item.title: Texas Industrial Facility Faces $3.5M in Fines for Safety Violations
item.description: Department of Labor has proposed more than $3.5 million in fines against three companies after federal inspectors determined they failed to protect workers during post-emergency response cleanup after a chemical spill at the BWC Terminals industrial facility east of Houston, Texas. faces $392,501 in proposed penalties for two willful and five serious violations that include a lack of training, a safety and health program, an emergency response plan for hazardous waste operations and emergency response, and deficiencies related to use of respirators. 27, 2025, sulfuric acid spill at the BWC Terminals LLC industrial facility in Channelview led to multiple employee injuries.

item.title: ANV Agrees to Acquire Specialist MGA Assured Underwriting Group
item.description: ANV Group Holdings Ltd., a global insurance intermediary platform, announced it has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Assured Underwriting Group Ltd. We are confident this is the right home for our business, our people, and our clients.” In connection to the acquisition, Grant Thornton is serving as financial adviser for Assured Underwriting Group, while Clyde & Co is acting as AUG’s legal counsel to Assured Underwriting Group. (AUG), a specialist MGA providing insurance-backed financial protection products to tour operators and the travel sector across the United Kingdom and Europe.

item.title: Texas Supreme Court Rejects Lawsuit by Survivors of Uvalde School Shooting
item.description: The Texas Supreme Court on Friday rejected an appeal by Robb Elementary students and teachers who argued failures by the Department of Public Safety and Uvalde County contributed to a botched response to the 2022 shooting that killed 19 students and two teachers. All were traumatized.” The lawsuit argued that DPS and Uvalde County failed to protect students and teachers — an allegation, the court acknowledged, “we quite frankly cannot disagree with.” Even so, the appeals court said, their lawsuit argued that DPS and the county acted negligently under the Texas Tort Claims Act — a law that did not apply to their claims. The court’s order, issued without comment, upheld a February ruling by the San Antonio-based 4th Court of Appeals that dismissed the lawsuit.

item.title: Ageas UK Expands Distribution with New Home Insurance Partnership with Source Insurance
item.description: “We’re looking forward to building a strong relationship together and supporting Source in delivering smooth, customer-focused insurance solutions.” The post Ageas UK Expands Distribution with New Home Insurance Partnership with Source Insurance appeared first on Insurtech Insights . “Ageas brings great underwriting expertise, market presence and commitment to customer outcomes and we’re looking forward to working with them.” Strengthening Adviser Support The Source agreement builds on a series of recent distribution initiatives by Ageas UK, including an embedded insurance partnership with an original equipment manufacturer (OEM) insurer and support for a new embedded motor insurance proposition launched by electric vehicle manufacturer BYD. Claire Green, Distribution Director at Ageas UK, said the insurer is pleased to partner with Source as it continues to grow its home insurance panel.

item.title: What Insurance Leaders Get Wrong About Culture
item.description: When people think about a healthy culture in most insurance organizations, they usually say the same things: People follow the process, leaders avoid trouble, and long-tenured employees are promoted. Insurance leaders often mistake compliance for culture. When AI shifts what the work actually requires, carriers that have spent years measuring culture through stability metrics cannot answer basic questions: what does good behavior look like to support and sustain change in the organization and the industry overall?

item.title: Corgi launches Corgi Claims
item.description: Corgi announced the launch of Corgi Claims, a full-service third-party administrator. Corgi Claims handles every major line of business, including commercial liability, property, catastrophe, renters, trucking, workers’ compensation, and specialty programs. Corgi Claims is available for insurance carriers, MGAs, captives, program administrators, and self-insured organizations.

item.title: Bayer’s Supreme Court Win in Roundup Case No ‘Silver Bullet’
item.description: The US Supreme Court threw out a jury verdict won by a Missouri man who blamed Roundup for his non-Hodgkin lymphoma. “It should never happen again.” While the Supreme Court decision is a big win for Bayer, Anderson has yet to secure final approval for his $7.25 billion settlement proposal. After years of legal setbacks that have cost billions of dollars over its top-selling Roundup weedkiller, Bayer AG finally got a resounding win in the biggest tribunal of all.

item.title: Supreme Court Rejects Trump Appeal of Carroll Sex-Abuse Verdict
item.description: The verdict is one of two Carroll won against Trump, who has indicated he also will seek Supreme Court review of an $83.3 million award in a separate defamation suit. Carroll also said Trump defamed her when he posted on social media in 2022 that her allegations were a “complete con job,” a “Hoax” and a “Scam.” In that same post, Trump said that Carroll “is not my type!” The 2nd US Circuit Court of Appeals upheld the verdict, saying the testimony of the other alleged victims and the Access Hollywood recording helped establish a pattern of conduct by Trump. Carroll urged the Supreme Court not to hear the case, saying the evidentiary issues presented by the appeal weren’t worthy of high court review.

item.title: Older Buildings, Substandard Construction Left Venezuela Vulnerable to Earthquakes
item.description: Older buildings, substandard construction, and geography left many neighborhoods in Venezuela vulnerable to strong earthquakes like the ones that struck the country this week. Since the 1970s, engineers have known that concrete buildings are particularly susceptible to earthquakes and seek to reinforce new construction with steel. Engineers and other experts said the back-to-back earthquakes on Wednesday were among the most intense to hit the country in more than a century, leveling buildings and leaving more than 900 dead, with the number expected to rise.

item.title: Virtual outreach event on FSB sound practices for financial institutions’ responsible adoption of artificial intelligence
item.description: On 7 July 2026 at 13:00 CEST the FSB will host a virtual event as part of the consultation process for its proposed sound practices for financial institutions’ responsible adoption of AI. On 10 June 2026, the Financial Stability Board published a consultation report on Sound Practices for Responsible Adoption of Artificial Intelligence (AI) to help financial institutions responsibly navigate AI adoption in a rapidly evolving technological landscape. As part of its consultation, the FSB will host a virtual outreach event on Tuesday 7 July from 13:00-15:00 CEST .

item.title: Refining the Consumer Duty to give greater confidence to wholesale firms
item.description: We have heard from some firms that uncertainty about the Duty causes friction in wholesale markets. It’s a consumer duty not a wholesale one. We are clear that where firms don't genuinely shape consumer outcomes, the Duty doesn't apply.

item.title: Crunchbase Data: Q2 Brought The Most Billion-Dollar Startup Exits Since 2021
item.description: Startup exits valued at $1 billion or more are now more numerous than at any point since the 2021 market peak, Crunchbase data shows. This period has brought us both the largest venture-backed exit of all time, with SpaceX , and a bevy of other comparatively tinier but still sizable startup exits through acquisition or IPO. SpaceX’s $60 billion acquisition of AI coding platform Cursor a few days after its IPO, for instance, was the priciest purchase of a private, venture-backed startup ever.

item.title: Demex launches modelled-loss trigger aggregate winter storm reinsurance product
item.description: The Demex Group, a risk analytics and intelligence company that facilitates climate and catastrophe peril parametric reinsurance protection, has announced the launch of a new modelled-loss trigger winter storm reinsurance product for primary carriers. This new winter storm reinsurance product is based on Demex’s working-layer, aggregate reinsurance solution for severe convective storm product that was launched a few years ago. Demex said that its winter storm product “uses a carrier’s ground-up claims data from the past decade and overlays corresponding weather data to develop a sophisticated modeled loss index.” Reinsurers then offer limit to carriers based on the index to meet their specific risk transfer needs.

item.title: As importance of portfolio construction rises, reinsurance put in focus by KKR
item.description: Reinsurance as an asset class now deserves a place among KKR’s latest Capital Market Assumptions (CMAs), with the investment giant explaining that portfolio construction and private market assets are rising in importance, as a broad beta approach to investing is less appropriate at this stage of the economic, financial and capital market cycle. KKR has long had an appetite for deriving returns from insurance and reinsurance opportunities, and as an asset class, or industry capital can be deployed in, re/insurance has regularly featured in its insights. But, as financial markets remain volatile and the tailwinds that have driven recent historical performance slow, KKR is elevating reinsurance into a more prominent position in its thought-leadership, it seems.

item.title: Relation Insurance Services expands Specialty Practice with senior hires
item.description: Relation Insurance Services, an insurance brokerage firm, has confirmed the appointment of Matthew Schott, Kevin Smith, Courtney Maugé, Jonathan Franznick and Scott Moseley to its Specialty Practice. Douglas Turk, President of Specialty at Relation Insurance Services, said: “Matt, Kevin, Courtney, Jonathan and Scott are incredibly talented experts who have exceptional reputations in their respective specialties.” “Their addition strengthens our capabilities across key specialty disciplines serving complex risk and reflects the broader momentum we are building across the Specialty platform. We are building a destination for top industry talent who are empowered to work with clients and markets to deliver exceptional results.” Relation Insurance Services noted that the appointments form part of its continued focus on developing its specialty offering and strengthening a platform that combines technical capability with national scale.

item.title: Mayflower and Hadron launch US dedicated affirmative AI liability program
item.description: Mayflower Specialty, Ltd., a New York-based managing general agent, and Hadron, a multinational specialty carrier, have launched the first dedicated affirmative artificial intelligence liability program in the United States. Underwritten by Mayflower and issued through Hadron, these policies provide explicit coverage across the full management-liability triad: errors and omissions (E&O), employment practices liability (EPL), and directors and officers (D&O) coverage. Mayflower was built to close that gap.” Coverage is structured as affirmative grants for management liability, employment practices, and professional liability arising from AI deployment, paired with a difference-in-conditions and excess layer that drops down where legacy policies are silent, sub-limited, or exclusionary.

item.title: William Fawcett appointed Head of ReSources at Reinsurance Association of America
item.description: The Reinsurance Association of America (RAA), the trade association representing property and casualty reinsurers operating in the United States, has announced the appointment of William Fawcett as Head of ReSources and the RAA Reinsurance Education Institute. His experience across the global reinsurance and insurance sector will enhance the educational resources we provide to the industry and policymakers.” According to the RAA, Fawcett has also held senior executive positions with international organisations including Allianz Global, AXA, Flagstone Reinsurance Holdings SA and Haverford Holdings, where he contributed to the development and leadership of high-performing teams. In his new role, Fawcett will oversee initiatives designed to strengthen and expand the RAA’s position in industry education, while also supporting the growth and diversification of the association’s revenue streams.

item.title: Italy’s Former Railways Chief Starts 5-Year Jail Term for 2009 Disaster
item.description: The 72-year-old former head of Italy’s state railway company has begun a five-year jail term over a 2009 derailment that killed 32 people, in a case highlighting the issue of management liability and the snail’s pace of Italian justice. After a lengthy initial trial, Moretti was given a seven-year jail term in 2017. He did not go to prison because sentences in Italy are rarely effective before the appeals process is completed.

item.title: Supreme Court Rejects Bid to Review SEC’s Rescinded ‘Gag Rule’
item.description: The US Supreme Court refused to review the constitutionality of a now-rescinded Securities and Exchange Commission policy that forced people and companies settling enforcement cases to never publicly criticize or contest the Wall Street watchdog’s claims. The justices without comment on Monday turned away a constitutional challenge over whether the SEC’s so-called gag rule violated individuals’ rights. The SEC enacted the so-called “gag rule” in 1972.

item.title: Neptune Doubles Flood Policy Limits to $15 Million
item.description: Neptune Insurance, one of the largest private flood insurance providers, has doubled its policy limits on most types of coverage, including limits of up to $15 million on commercial and residential structures, the company announced. Related: Kentucky Hit Again by Deadly Flooding After Heavy Rains Neptune Reports Profit Drop But Strong Growth in Policy Counts After IPO Flood Insurance Gap Will Squeeze Local Governments and Property Owners, Moody’s Says Top photo: Fort Lauderdale flooding in 2023 (AP Photo/Rebecca Blackwell, File) The move gives Neptune’s nationwide network of 45,000 insurance agents more flexibility in writing flood coverage, as takeup on National Flood Insurance Program policies remains relatively low for many parts of the country.

item.title: The Doctors Company Completes $1.3 Billion Acquisition of ProAssurance
item.description: The Doctors Company has completed its $1.3 billion acquisition of medical malpractice insurer ProAssurance, creating the second-largest provider of medical professional liability insurance in the United States. The transaction, first announced in 2025, saw The Doctors Company acquire all outstanding shares of Birmingham, Alabama-based ProAssurance for $25 per share, valuing the company at approximately $1.3 billion. The acquisition strengthens The Doctors Company’s position in the medical malpractice insurance market, placing it behind only Berkshire Hathaway’s MedPro Group in terms of market size.

item.title: Sixth Street Acquires Majority Stake in Monument Re to Support Long-Term Growth
item.description: Global investment firm Sixth Street has agreed to acquire a majority stake in Monument Re, providing fresh capital to support the pan-European life insurance consolidator’s long-term strategic growth. The transaction, announced by Sixth Street, will see investment vehicles managed or advised by the firm become Monument Re’s majority shareholder, while Hannover Re will retain a significant equity stake and continue supporting the business as both a shareholder and reinsurance partner. We look forward to fulfilling Monument’s potential as a leading consolidator in its core markets, while maintaining a focus on long-term security for policyholders,” said Rohan Singhal, Partner and Head of Insurance at Sixth Street.

item.title: QBE Strengthens Asia Leadership with COO and AI Appointments
item.description: Global insurer QBE has strengthened its leadership team in Asia with the appointment of a new Chief Operating Officer and a regional Head of Data, Analytics and AI, reinforcing its commitment to operational excellence, digital transformation and responsible artificial intelligence across the region. Supporting Regional Growth Commenting on the appointments, Asia Chief Executive Rob Kosova said the new executives would play a key role in strengthening QBE’s competitive position across the region. The appointments reflect QBE’s continued investment in technology, operational transformation and artificial intelligence as insurers across Asia increasingly leverage data and AI to improve underwriting, customer experience and business performance.

item.title: Doubtless officially launches as JAB’s global pet care brand
item.description: Doubtless , the new global pet care company created by combining Independence Pet Holdings and Pinnacle Pet Group, officially launched today with a mission to improve access to pet care and help pets live healthier, happier lives. According to the company, the launch marks the next phase of building a more connected global pet care business by aligning operations, integrating capabilities, and expanding its offerings beyond insurance. By harnessing the power of our international footprint, we are building on our leadership in pet insurance to create a global suite of solutions for pet parents, veterinarians and industry partners that makes pet care easier to access, navigate and manage.” – CEO Dirk Beeckman.

item.title: HUB submits draft registration statement for proposed initial public offering
item.description: Securities and Exchange Commission relating to the proposed initial public offering of its common stock. Insurance broker Hub International Holdings announced that it has confidentially submitted a draft registration statement on Form S-1 with the U.S. The timing, number of shares of common stock to be offered and the price range for the proposed offering have not yet been determined.

item.title: Sixth Street to become majority shareholder of Monument Re
item.description: Global investment firm Sixth Street announced that investment vehicles managed or advised by Sixth Street have entered into a definitive agreement to acquire a majority stake in Monument Re, a pan-European insurance consolidator, and support the company’s long-term strategic growth plan. Hannover Re will continue as a key shareholder and remains committed to Monument and Sixth Street’s strategy for the business moving forward. We look forward to fulfilling Monument’s potential as a leading consolidator in its core markets, while maintaining a focus on long-term security for policyholders.” – Rohan Singhal, Partner and Head of Insurance at Sixth Street.

item.title: How Insurers Know When It’s Time to Scale AI
item.description: Instead, they’re grappling with a different question: How do organizations know when an AI pilot is ready to scale? “If they’re talking about the outcomes that they’re driving toward, the expectation of what the impact is going to be, the change on the processes inside of the business, that’s when you know that they’re ready to scale. If they’re still talking about it from a pure technology perspective or the models that they’re interested in or the theory and concepts behind AI, you know that they’re a long way off.” Related: Mythos Myths: Good Guys Hold More Cybersecurity Cards, Insurer CEO Says Thom said that distinction is critical because insurers can easily become distracted by technically impressive solutions that deliver limited value.

item.title: US DFC And World Bank’s MIGA Setting Up Political Risk Insurance for Ukraine Fund
item.description: International Development Finance Corporation has agreed with the World Bank’s guarantee arm MIGA to create a political risk insurance framework for a U.S.-Ukrainian reconstruction investment fund. reduce the risk so that the private sector can feel confident about the opportunity to invest in Ukraine’s future,” World Bank President Ajay Banga said at the signing. Ed Mountfield, vice president and chief financial officer of the World Bank’s Multilateral Investment Guarantee Agency, who signed the deal with Coleman, said the war-risk coverage would help mobilize private capital investments and strengthen Ukraine’s overall business environment.

item.title: The 24-Hour Window: The Race to Keep Claims Out of Litigation
item.description: It is also a critical window to reach claimants before confusion, frustration or attorney influence changes the direction of the claim. Early contact can shorten claim cycles and reduce the likelihood that issues escalate into costly disputes or litigation. In insurance, the first 24 hours after a claim is filed are often the most important.

item.title: Marsh New Zealand promotes Gabe Money to risk consulting leader
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Global property catastrophe rates fall to minus 16% at 1.7 renewals: Guy Carpenter
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Japan’s direct premiums grew 3.8% to US$66.1bn in FY25: GIAJ
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Non-UK business removed from Consumer Duty scope to reduce burdens on wholesale businesses
item.description: Wholesale financial businesses involved in retail markets will find it easier to comply with the Consumer Duty, following proposals from the FCA. Under the proposals, firms will benefit from: Simon Walls, executive director of markets, said: 'The Consumer Duty is helping deliver good outcomes and build confidence for retail consumers, but it was never intended to become a Wholesale Duty imposing on deals between sophisticated parties. That's why we are refining its scope to provide greater clarity to wholesale markets, and keep the focus on the consumer outcomes it was created to improve.' The FCA has today also published proposals to further simplify its insurance rules, while maintaining appropriate levels of consumer protection.

item.title: Leadenhall’s Tranquil Re 2026-1 cat bond for Nectaris Re could upsize to $75m
item.description: – Tranquil Re 2026-1 catastrophe bond could upsize to provide as much as $75 million of US named storm and earthquake protection for the ILS manager backed rated reinsurance platform Nectaris Re, while the price guidance for the notes has now been lowered, Artemis understands. Earlier in June, we reported that Nectaris Re Ltd., the rated reinsurance platform backed by ILS manager Leadenhall Capital Partners LLP, was targeted to receive its first catastrophe bond protection from this Tranquil Re 2026-1 cat bond. Now targeted to upsize, the $70 million to $75 million of Tranquil Re 2026-1 Class A notes are designed to provide Nectaris Re Ltd.

item.title: Hannover Re returns for more North American retro with $125m 3264 Re 2026-1 cat bond
item.description: This new 3264 Re Series 2026-1 catastrophe bond is the first under the catastrophic peril focused program this year and sees Hannover Re again looking to bolster its North American retro protection. Last year, Hannover Re tapped the cat bond market three times for nat cat protection, $200 million of worldwide peak peril retro through the 3264 Re 2025-1 cat bond, then $150 million of US and Canadian cover in May through the 3264 Re 2025-2 offering and finally another $250 million of North American peak peril retrocession through a 3264 Re Ltd. Global reinsurer Hannover Re is back in the catastrophe bond market and looking to secure additional North American peak peril retrocessional reinsurance, with a $125 million initial target for a new 3264 Re Ltd.

item.title: Property reinsurance softening accelerates at mid-year amid capital growth, ILS expansion: Guy Carpenter
item.description: Guy Carpenter also emphasised that the abundance of capital is keeping the property reinsurance market soft, with risk-adjusted decreases further deepening from levels seen at the January 1st renewals. Guy Carpenter, the global reinsurance broking firm, has reported that a combination of abundant capacity and a growing appetite from reinsurers has kept the competitive pricing environment intact at the mid-year renewals, while also highlighting that current market conditions are encouraging buyers to explore alternative options such as parametric solutions and sidecars to complement traditional catastrophe programmes. In its July 2026 reinsurance renewals report, the broker states that within property lines, attractive terms and coverage options are spurring exploration of supplemental solutions to augment traditional catastrophe programs.

item.title: Sixth Street to acquire majority stake in Monument Re
item.description: Sixth Street, a global investment firm, has announced that investment vehicles it manages or advises have entered into a definitive agreement to acquire a majority stake in Monument Re, a life insurance and reinsurance holding company. Global reinsurer Hannover Re will remain a key shareholder and is committed to supporting Monument and Sixth Street’s strategy for the business. Rohan Singhal, Partner and Head of Insurance at Sixth Street, said, “We are excited to partner with Monument and Hannover Re as we expand our global insurance footprint.

item.title: Dave Draper named CUO for UK and Active Underwriter of Syndicate 1221
item.description: US-based insurer The Hartford has appointed Dave Draper as the Chief Underwriting Officer for the UK and Active Underwriter at The Hartford Underwriting Agency, Ltd – Syndicate 1221, subject to regulatory approvals. – Syndicate 1221, said, “I’m pleased to announce that Dave Draper has been appointed Chief Underwriting Officer (UK) & Active Underwriter (subject to regulatory approval). “Following a strong tenure as interim CUO driving underwriting discipline, performance, and strategic delivery, Dave will now lead our underwriting organisation on a permanent basis.” Draper is an insurance industry veteran with four decades of experience, having started his career at Builders’ Accident Insurance Company Ltd in 1986 as an Assistant Underwriter.

item.title: Demex launches “first-of-its-type” winter storm reinsurance product
item.description: Demex, a technology-enabled reinsurance solution provider, has announced a new winter storm product, to protect primary carriers from losses caused by unexpected storms, cold temperatures, and heavier than usual snowfall. Described as the first-of-its-type, this new reinsurance solution is based on Demex’s successful working-layer, aggregate reinsurance solution for severe convective storm losses. Matt Coleman, President, Reinsurance at Demex, commented: “It may seem odd launching a winter storm product in summer, but as JFK said, ‘The time to repair the roof is when the sun is shining.’ We’ve been proactively exploring how to extend our secondary peril reinsurance cover.

item.title: Debt, AI Boom and Financial Fragilities Raise Global Risks, BIS Says
item.description: Global pressures from rising public debt to financial fragilities and the sustainability of the AI boom are increasing risks, underscoring the need for disciplined policymaking, according to the Bank for International Settlements. “Policy actions must reinforce each other to avoid a pull and push on the global economy. Ultimately, success depends on sound fiscal and financial foundations,” BIS General Manager Pablo Hernandez de Cos said.

item.title: Extreme Heat Grips Eastern Europe After Records Fall in Germany
item.description: The most extreme heat has subsided in western Europe after breaking temperature records in France, where health authorities reported more than 1,000 excess deaths. The deadly heat wave that’s set temperature records across western Europe for more than a week has shifted east to scorch Hungary, Romania and the Balkans. Red warnings for extreme heat have been issued in Poland, Hungary, Romania, Croatia, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Serbia and Slovakia.

item.title: Hormuz Traffic Drops Off as Vessel Attacks Raise Fresh Concerns
item.description: Commercial traffic through the Strait of Hormuz persisted at a reduced level after attacks on two ships raised fresh concerns about the ability and willingness of shipowners to traverse the waterway. Following the two vessel attacks, there were tit-for-tat strikes between the US and Iran over the weekend that tested their fragile truce, though the two sides have since agreed to halt their assaults before negotiations resume this week. Some vessels that recently abandoned crossings, including two laden VLCCs and an inbound Qatar-owned gas carrier, have not made fresh attempts following the attacks.

item.title: Aviva to rebrand Probitas 1492 as ‘Aviva Syndicates’
item.description: Aviva will rebrand Lloyd’s syndicate Probitas 1492 as “Aviva Syndicates” at the end of September, completing the integration of its Lloyd’s platform into Aviva’s Global Corporate & Specialty business. Aviva acquired Probitas in 2024 as part of a strategy to build a dual-stamp specialty business under a single brand. “Bringing Probitas fully under the Aviva brand is the next step in building a truly integrated specialty business.

item.title: ‘Sweet spot for global insurance’: regulatory confidence key to attracting global insurers to Asia
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: CorVel launches market-based medical bill repricing solution
item.description: CorVel Corporation has introduced Marketwise Repricing, a new medical bill repricing solution designed to help employers, insurers, and third-party administrators reduce medical costs by applying market-based reimbursement methodologies within existing bill review programs. The Fort Worth-based risk management and workers’ compensation services provider said the solution addresses shortcomings in traditional network and repricing approaches, which may not accurately reflect local market conditions due to variations in medical billing across states, providers, and care settings. According to CorVel, Marketwise Repricing uses real-world reimbursement data to deliver pricing that better aligns with local market dynamics and is particularly useful for out-of-network claims and jurisdictions with limited or no medical fee schedules.
